I loved my apartment. It felt homely with a well-considered decor. I share the same taste with the designer. Down to the selection of art work. review_title: A wonderful location, but did not live up to expectations.
review_text:
The dinner in the restaurant was very disappointing. Only one of the vegetarian options was available. The advertised price for dinner for those staying in the restaurant had gone up and was a single fixed menu which did not appeal to us, so we ate a la carte. We were twice presented with an incorrect bill - incorrect as to what we had and as to price. We eventually managed to get it right but this caused confusion for our final bill when checking out. The price I had been quoted for our upgraded room was exceeded in the final bill and the receptionist refused to change it.There is no bar in the hotel except during the limited hours that the restaurant is open. You can buy cold drinks at reception, but coming back on a very wet afternoon there was no possibility of obtaining a hot drink which I think there should have been. Do as we did and take a travel kettle and supplies.Cleanliness: In the breakfast area of the dining room there was a pedal bin which had not been emptied for at least two days and which had fruit flies multiplying inside it.Safety: the wooden flooring of the balcony was extremely slippery when wet as it had not been cleared of algae.This could, and should be a lovely hotel, but these things spoiled it.,

Location beside the lake was wonderful. We upgraded to a room with balcony which I would advise.

review_title: It's ok just for one night, but we wouldn't come back
review_text:
When we arrived at the hotel, at around 6 p.m., we didn't find anyone welcoming us at the reception: there were only different keys on the table for different guests (to be honest, anyone could have entered the hotel and picked them up from the table because there was literally no one...). In fact, we picked up our key and at first we stepped on the wrong strairs, so we had to go search for our room (it would have been useful to know this information from the receptionist).When we entered the room and the bathroom, we could tell that they were clean: too bad that when we opened the toilet lid, there was some toilet paper in the WC (maybe it was a mistake, but it was disgusting to see it).The worst thing was that, after taking a shower, a smell of sewer came out of the shower (we did not feel at ease after this).The next morning my boyfriend kindly explained in French to the owners of the hotel (a young guy and a man) that we found strange not seeing anyone at the check-in, and they answered that it was our mistake because we said that we would have come in the morning (the check-in time is between 3-9 p.m., as shown on booking.com, so we thought it wouldn't have been a problem); anyway, the young guy was very nice with us and apologised, while the older one was not.We also kindly asked if it was possible to leave them the luggage for some time before leaving, and they said yes and that they would have been around all day, but when we came back to pick up the luggage no one was there.,

The hotel is in a very good position, it's exactly in the main square of Durbuy. The view is also very good, we could see the castle from our window. The room and the bathroom were clean and the breakfast was good, my boyfriend and I spent Easter in this hotel and we found different types of Easter eggs in the tray. It was very lovely!

review_text:
For us, and this may have only been associated with our room! As a couple, we always prefer a double bed rather than two singles pushed together! However, we did not request this during our booking so there is a good chance that they would honour this request should we have made it. Also, in our room the shower head seemed to be in the wrong place & soaked the floor. Neither was a massive problem for us & didn't dampen our enjoyment of the weekend break we had.,

Overall, the hotel was clean & in a great location for all the things we wanted to see in Bruges. The staff were courteous & helpful, whatever we asked of them they were happy to assist. The breakfast was tasty with enough choice & you can eat as much of the buffet as you wanted.

review_title: Nice accommodation for a weekend visit
review_text:
15-minute walk to the nearest tram stop (but once you're there, it's an easy ride to the city centre). On Booking.com it's described as being in Ghent, but it's actually in Melle. (If you put in the street address with "Ghent" into your car navigation system, you'll end up in the wrong place, further down the same road.) The stairs to the upstairs rooms are very narrow and very steep, so not recommended for people with mobility problems.,

Beautiful old-style house with very atmospheric breakfast room and calm countryside views. Just outside of Ghent (in Melle), but only a few kilometres away from the city centre, which is easy to each by tram. Very friendly staff and decent breakfast quality. We had one of the "family rooms" upstairs, which was very large and offered two separate sleeping areas with two single beds each. Free parking space on premises, so good for people who are travelling by car.

I would recommend this hotel easily.

i was in belgium for 12 days and although i travelled to other cities i booked accommodation only in brussels to avoid the hassle of moving hostels. imagine my surprise when i was forced to change rooms halfway through my stay (had to leave my room by midday store my stuff in luggage and come to a new room after 3pm basically checking out and back in later that day) and this was not communicated to me before i checked in - major inconvenience as i would not have stayed in brussels the entire time had i known this in time. on top of that the girl at front desk acted like she did me a favour by ensuring i didn't have to change rooms TWICE. first room was in an unincorporated area of the hostel only reachable through emergency stairs, no easy access to the kitchen, weird layout (two levels, stairs in weird places, sinks next to beds), unstable bunk beds, too few outlets and the only light was for the whole room. second room in the front part of the hostel was tiny and cramped, stuffy and the only window opened pretty much directly into the street. access to kitchen is very limited. wifi is spotty in parts and some of the staff is not particularly informative. the two parts of the hostel are completely separate which makes for a strange layout if you are in the wrong part.,

relatively close to both central and north station on foot or by traffic. well connected to public transport with stops for all metro lines as well as several buses and trams closeby. a number of attractions within walking distance including the congress column, botanical garden, brussels park, parliament, royal palace and grand place. a delhaize and carrefour store nearby as well as a couple of fast food places and pubs. overall in a good location.
the hostel itself is clean and well maintained. it has a lot of extra facilities including a luggage room and a well equipped kitchen, a cheap bar,  a balcony, a sitting area both inside and out, a laundry room and its own vending machines.  check in/check out is possible from early in the morning to late in the evening. staff speaks multiple languages and they are all very pleasant and polite. in collaboration with an agency a guide for a free walking tour picks up groups from the hostel twice a day which is very convenient. different free maps are available at front desk. there's free access to computers and the staff will print out your bus or plane tickets for free too.
the breakfast is great. there are a lot of different things to choose from, sweet and savory, different kinds of cured meat and cheese, eggs, butter, chocolate spreads, jams, different types of bread and milk (including soy milk) and cereal. there's always several types of fresh fruit. you can drink anything from water to juice to coffee to hot chocolate to all kinds of tea. the dining area has a toaster and a microwave. over a longer period it gets repetitive and it's not a replacement for a varied home-cooked diet, but it's a great hostel breakfast.
the rest of the experience depends largely on where in the hostel you are placed. my first room was very large and spacious with lots of windows, air and light. it was in a quiet part of the hostel overlooking a quiet street.

review_title: We would highly recommend a stay in this hotel
review_text:
The only downsides were that a few of the reception staff were not so friendly as Suhail, and we would have appreciated a bathmat in the bathroom. Nevertheless we would highly recommend a stay in this hotel.,

We enjoyed our stay at the  Century Hotel from the 12th to 18th February.  On arrival we received a very friendly welcome from Suhail. He explained that since we were early, he would check if a room was ready and asked if we would mind waiting for 5 – 10 mins. After a very short time he was able to give us a key card and told us that we would staying at the 9th floor where they had just fitted new showers and mattresses. The room was clean and tidy and the beds were very comfortable.The breakfast was particularly  good for a budget hotel:  good choice of hot breakfast items, healthy options, and nice Flemish breakfast items and plenty of fresh fruit.A little research shows that the hotel was built in the 1920s for the Antwerp World Exhibition in 1930. On the first floor there’s a long nice breakfast room overlooking the iconic central station.The hotel’s position in very central to the tourist and shopping attractions that Antwerp has to offer. The staff at breakfast and room cleaning were very attentive and friendly.

Everything was just perfect!,

1. The owners are great, always smiling, full of energy and they make you feel very welcome. 
2. The location is perfect: close to the train station and very close to the centre of Mons, in a very quiet area, on a narrow cobbled street.
3. The house is absolutely marvellous. The decor is vintage and elegant. The bathroom is astonishing, really stylish and special. The bed is comfortable, equipped with clean, high quality linen. 
4. The place is crystal clean. 
5. The breakfast was one of the best because it had lots of options, all of which were of extremely high quality (jams, cheese, cake, chocolate, butter, milk, yogurt, cereals, different types of bread, honey, coffee, orange and lemon juice, fruits).
Thank you for making our stay in Mons one we will never forget. ❤

The staff is not capable of solving any problems that you might encounter during your stay. They do not know how to operate gas heater, do not provide ironing board or iron. Cleaning is below average. Rooms have concrete floor and ceiling, and are very dark. 
Payment policy is the most ridiculous out of all places I’ve been. Property will block full price of your room few weeks in advance before free cancellation period is even over. Then they cancel it and do it again. And even after they had blocked the money, they require payment upfront. We were not able to check in until we paid for all our rooms. I had to pay for the room of one of my colleagues, and after she paid for her room from her credit card the next day, the hotel could not put money back on my card. Unless there are no other places available in Antwerpen, I recommend to look elsewhere, if you don’t want to ruin your vacation or business trip. ,

review_title: Loved the location but the stairs were a concern.
review_text:
This apartment is on 3 floors and the stairs are narrow and steep. Also the bedroom is at the top whilst the bathroom is on the ground floor.,

As we were exploring the WW1 battlefields this was the perfect place to stay. The Menin Gate was only a couple of minutes walk away and the rebuilt Cloth Hall and Cathedral are also very close.

Was in first floor and a little bit noisy because face to street.,

Room facilities, location, reasonable price

WiFi was not good because of the thick walls of the building but if you want to unplug it’s perfect. ,

The place was lovely, set in an old abbey I believe. You should opt to eat here as the food is fantastic and fresh each day. Staff, family run, were very nice and helpful. It’s in a good location to explore the region and was good to come back to hotel for a great dinner each night. The location is just outside Malmedy so you need a car or bicycle to visit town center.

This is a huge hotel, which meant that the elevators and breakfast room were always quite busy and crammed. The building is also a bit old and didn't look like it had been renovated recently-there was also a weird, musty smell around the corridors, at least on the third floor. There are predominantly dark colors, such as deep purple, on the walls and carpets (since there are pictures of actors everywhere, perhaps to resemble a movie theater as a theme?), which makes the inside look a bit gloomy at times. We asked a member of the staff how much a taxi ride to the airport would cost and were told 20-25 euros depending on traffic, whereas the next day, another member of the staff told us it would cost 40-45 euros (it eventually cost 49 euros). Even though these are not major problems, we might choose a more southern point in the city for any future visits, as the area around Marivaux seemed a bit less lively and somewhat rundown (however this is clearly a matter of personal preference).,

We were pleasantly surprised to find how spacious the room was! We also really liked the bed mattresses, which were exactly as firm as they need to be after walking around all day. The room had most probably been renovated and was quite clean. It also came with many amenities such as an espresso machine and espresso capsules, slippers, notepad and pencil, safe, refrigerator, and hangers, all of which were really useful. We particularly appreciated the small, complimentary bottles of water. The desk and closets were also rather big and convenient and there was more than enough room for our luggage. The bathroom was also spacious, with a separate bathtub plus a corner shower in one room and a toilet in a different room. The staff were always very polite and friendly, we couldn't open the refrigerator upon arrival and they fixed it the next morning. Last but not least, the hotel is right next to a subway stop (Rogier) and roughly 12 minutes away from Grand Place on foot.

review_title: Great location, excellent host, stunning rooms!

We took the scenic route down the canal opposite to walk into town, which took a little longer but we managed to view a lot of the sights before hitting the Christmas Markets. We stayed in the L Suite and the room was truly stunning, Jo is an interior designer and he has styled some amazing rooms. The handmade bath was beautiful & so relaxing- hands down one of the most luxurious rooms we have stayed in. The beds were also super comfy & the place was extremely clean.We can't recommend Kind of OJ enough, if you are thinking of booking, you will not be disappointed!

review_title: Decent hotel in a bad part of town
review_text:
Carpets in general are always outdated and unhygenic, but this one was covered in unknown stains, all across the room. Reading other people's reviews I realised I was not the only one encountering this. I have never seen something like this in Holland. This is unacceptable for a (4-star) hotel. (see photo)While walking around outside its easy to forget that you're actually in Europe; the hotel is close to a train station; at night there are a lot of drug addicts, homeless and gipsies on the streets, sleeping outside or talking to you if you walk by. They come in big numbers. If you travel by car, especially one having a ''foreign'' license plate, it is highly advised to park it in the hotel garage. This costs you €25. Group of men hang around the parked cars outside, I witnessed one urinating against one. I personally wouldn't go out alone at night as a woman or with children. Be aware of pickpockets too.,

Rooms were very quiet, unlike many others hotels in Brussels. Reception staff spoke Dutch like they're supposed to.Overall, I've learnt that booking a hotel is always quite a gamble in Belgium, even at 4-5 star hotels, the quality standard is lower. However this one was decent enough.

First of all, I was impressed with your helpfulness and kindness upon my arrival, and with the way you made sure everything was just right. The biggest problem, however, was one of understaffing. The receptionist was friendly, but clearly overworked. Besides her, the only other person around was the bar lady, whose first day it was. She had clearly not received sufficient training and could not cope with the pressure.  There was no manager around to assist or to deal with guest complaints. There is no service on the terrace (which is a nice place to sit) so you have to queue at the bar and hope for the best. review_title: Wow!  Amazing property and hosts - wish I could give higher than a 10!
review_text:
No complaints, everything was perfect!,

This accommodation was truly amazing and one of the nicest places we have seen, a real home from home.  We stayed 3 nights but would love to have stayed for 3 weeks!  It is a cosy, self-contained property with its own front door and has a bedroom, bathroom, kitchen and lounge/diner.  Parking is available right outside the door.  The rooms are very spacious and nicely decorated/furnished and the brick ceilings give it a lot of character.  The double bed is very comfortable, blackout blinds keep out the light and the area is silent at night so you get a good nights sleep.  The kitchen is well equipped with microwave, kettle, toaster, fridge, electric hob and all the crockery and utensils you could need.  A breakfast bar makes a handy place to sit and have a drink or a quick snack.  The lounge is very comfortable and a dining table with four chairs is a great place to enjoy a good meal.  The whole property was spotlessly clean, the wifi was good and there are plenty of electric sockets.  The hosts, Virginie and Eric, are lovely people, so friendly and helpful.  They left us a lot of information on the area and were happy to answer any questions that we had.  One of the best parts was the wood stove and free firewood, and Virginie & Eric came in each evening and lit it for us so it was warm and cosy when we arrived back.  The location is great, on the edge of a small village but only a few minutes away from shops and supermarkets.  It is less than 5 minutes from the autoroute which gives quick and easy connections to other parts of Belgium.  A perfect base for exploring Wallonia with plenty to see in the area, and only 20 mins from Brussels Charleroi airport.  We would love to return some day!

review_title: A great home to stay in in a lovely and calm residential area
review_text: Lovely house in a lovely residential area. Well furnished and well equipped. Lots of space inside and a large patio with a jacuzzi. Garage with a sauna and bicycles if needed. Nice to have a kitchen with an induction hob, dishwasher and a huge freezer too. All those little things you wonder if you need to bring were there, decent sized toiletries, soap, washing-up liquid, dishwasher tabs, loads of towels, salt, kitchen towel, kitchen roll... Internet connection worked fine for streaming and there is a strong wifi signal throughout the property. Owners easily reachable on whatsapp and they live next door. They're experienced hosts with more than one property so clearly know what they're doing. Would definitely look to stay here again next time we're in the region.

review_title: Old Tom - a perfect stay, as always,
review_text:
I am never disappointed with Old Tom. I enjoy staying there, and appreciate it for being a small well run hotel.,

I have stayed at Old Tom on a number of occasions over the past 25 years, and will stay there again when I next visit Ypres. For me Old Tom is perfect - excellent location, staff and restaurant. The restaurant has been redeveloped over the years and is very "light" and has a good feel about it. The accommodation remains the same as it has always been, and although some may consider the bedrooms to be "old" I think of it as querky and very fitting for the building and its history. The bedrooms are comfortable and clean and have everything needed for a short stay. If you are looking for a 5* hotel experience then go stay in a 5* hotel. If you are looking for a friendly, small, well run, comfortable hotel that serves very good food and is ideally located on the town square, then Old Tom is perfect. I shall continue to return.

I booked this apartment 5 weeks in advance, and at that time I had a lot of options. I only wanted two things: 1) a place near the train station and 2) air conditioning. This apartment advertised both. Imagine my surprise when I got there and the air conditioner was broken. The apartment owner claimed to not have known it was broken, but somehow I figured it out in 5 minutes. You better believe she charged me a premium for having an "air conditioner" too. I complained to Booking.com and the apartment owner told them that she would have it fixed first thing in the morning. Never happened. All I can say is if you claim to have certain amenities, you better have them. I was deceived/lied to, and there's nothing I can do about it. This bad review doesn't compare to my hot and sweaty 5 days in that dump, but it's all I've got.

They have alot of things which can be needed for regular traveller such as cloth hangers, hair dryer, shampoo, soaps etc. We just suprised that there wasnt daily usage sandals (usually white color) but the floor was covered with carpet and it wasnt a problem.

Great location, friendly staff. Would definitely recommend.

review_title: Lovely B&B in a historic building
review_text: Maison Mielou was a lovely place to stay. The room was beautiful and spacious and the host very nice. Please check in advance if they have availability should you need parking as they only have one spot for guests. However there was free street parking about 700m away. The bed was comfortable and the room lovely but it is an old house and there are creaky floor boards and quite thin walls. You definitely heard it when your partner used the bathroom at night! The interior is very lush and a bit eccentric. They even provided candles for a romantic ambiance. :-) Also note that the breakfast is not included in room price and it is served in the room. We found it relaxing to enjoy our breakfast in our pyjamas in the comfort of our room but the choice is limited to what's on the tray. It was a good quality continental breakfast (croissant, bread rolls, cheese, jam, brioche, boiled egg, yoghurt, fresh fruit), the first morning more generous than on our second day. There was also coffee and tea making possibility in the room. The house itself is gorgeous but the neighborhood is not the best. But it was an easy 20min walk to the city center. Maison Mielou also has bicycles to use. We did that and thought it was a great way of getting around and exploring also the outskirts of town. There are bicycle lanes everywhere and cars are used to bikes so it was very easy and enjoyable. Overall a good experience and I would stay Maison Mielou again.

The hotel is located smack in the center of the "lower-part" Charleroi which has been renovated in recent years, close to the station. it is integrated in a big shopping mall but the entrance is just outside the mall, you have to take an elevator to get to the reception. Rooms are nice and comfy.

He eventually moved us to one of those rooms but it was still dirty with mould inside the cabinet in the bathroom and towels you could shoot peas through.  There were glass rings on the furniture which was covered in dust and the state of the radiators was sickening. I took a video or the first room and a couple of photos of the second room. We lived out of our suitcases for the 4 days & we were there on our own there were no other guests.I wouldn't recommend this dirty hotel run by a very rude owner/manager to anyone!,

review_title: Perfect location, beautiful and quaint guesthouse.
review_text: The location of this beautiful guesthouse was perfect for exploring Bruges, which in itself is a magical place. review_title: Beautiful view, nice staff, good breakfast, good location, would definitely stay again.
review_text:
A general dearth of plug sockets-one on only one side of the bed, one at an awkward waist-height location with no table nearby, and one in the bathroom. The bathroom also had a see-through door. It was blurry glass, so you couldn't see everything, but it's more than clear enough to see the general shape of your partner sitting on the throne on his ipad. Not to mention, the sound is clear enough to hear through clearly. Kills the romantic vibe somewhat! The coffee tasted like instant, and while the room itself was beautiful, the ground floor hallway looks very scruffy and needs a refurb. I requested a certain room well ahead of time for the cosy aesthetic and the beautiful canal view, and although the staff were able to give it to us, they were unable to confirm it until the day of arrival. As the rooms were not ridiculously expensive but definitely not cheap, I would have been very annoyed to turn up and find out I couldn't have the room and that I had to pay over the odds for a generic-looking hotel room when I could have gone elsewhere for cheaper.,

I requested the ground floor ahead of time and the staff were kind enough to give it to us. The view over the canal, bridges and architecture from this room is beautiful-we did not know until we did a walking tour but the hotel used in the film In Bruges is next door, so the canal we could see from the window was the same canal Colin Farrell jumped into. There are frequently swans and ducks floating about under the window, which was nice. The room was toasty warm at all times even when it was freezing outside, which is great for a basement/ground floor, very clean, the shower was good and there was also a bathtub. There was a lot of lighting in the room-over each side of the bed, the whole bed, the whole room, the table area, the sofa-so you can pick whether you want romantic lighting, reading lights, general light, etc. The bed was huge, and very comfortable. My partner who is a bad sleeper found it excellent for sleeping undisturbed by my moving about or my (alleged) snoring. There is also a sofa which converts to another bed, a small table with two chairs, and another smaller sofa built in to an alcove. Despite all this, there is plenty of floor space because the room is pretty massive. The breakfast was pretty good and varied. There are some lovely bars and squares nearby if you exit and head away from the main square, as well as a good bottle shop, lots of chocolate shops, the torture museum, pretty bridges and a hotel bar (though we didn't go in, it looked good). The wifi was also very good.

review_title: Lovely B&B within easy walking distance of Bruges centre.
review_text:
Everything was fine.,

We had a lovely large room at the b&b with a very comfortable bed. The room was very clean and quiet. The bathroom was large and there were plenty of towels and toiletries.We were able to park outside the house with a parking permit but you can park quite nearby without needing a permit.We stayed for one night and were the only people staying there. We were able to use the downstairs sitting area to relax when we got back from Bruges centre. There was tea and coffee available whenever we wanted it and there was a very reasonably priced honesty bar.We had a very nice  continental breakfast in the morning with fresh croissants, bread, cheese, ham and jam.The hosts are very helpful and pleasant and showed us round the small brewery that they run next door to the house. It took us about 20 mins to walk to Bruges centre either through the park or through some small streets. Both routes were very pretty.The b&b itself is a lovely old-style house in a residential street. It is beautifully kept and very comfortable.

review_text:

We stayed in room 4 on the 2nd floor and the first 2 nights were amazing, but the last night we noticed so many mosquitos in our room. No idea where they were coming from but we killed so many and caused us not to sleep well. Woke up the next morning with a lot of bites on both my husband and I. I ended going through the reviews and saw that at least 1 other person had the same complaint. Other than the mosquitos, I enjoyed they stay.,

I loved the location and convenience of the property. We parked at the interpark within a 5 minute walk and our room was on the 2nd floor. Stairs were not a problem for us but could be for others, there is no elevator. Our tv worked, A/C worked, and our room was clean. Complimentary water and hairdryer saved the day! There is a great Italian restaurant right next door. I loved how innovative the key pickup and drop off was.
Breakfast of 3 items is difficult to understand. The price of the food itself probably plays minor role in the total cost. So why to have this complicated system?

review_title: Excellent location, spacious and the garage is a bonus
review_text:
It would have been nice to have had a double bed rather than two single beds pushed together. It would also be good if there was a proper oven at the property rather than a portable oven which did work adequately but not sure if we would want to cook a roast dinner in it plus the manual isn't  in English which made it tricky. There are also several flights of stairs at the property which would potentially be tricky for less mobile individuals.,

The location of the property is excellent as it is a few minutes walk from the centre of Bruges and as it is down a side street the noise is minimal. The property has a garage which is ideal if you are travelling by car; we have an Audi a4 and it was big enough to accomodate this. The property is very spacious and there was plenty of room for the two of us! All of the rooms were of a good clean size and it was very handy having a kitchen which meant we didn't have to eat out. The property is warm as we travelled in December to the Christmas market so we had to use the central heating. Nadia met us at the property on arrival and her local knowledge was helpful. We would definitely stay here again without a doubt.

review_title: Good apartment depending on what you are looking for.
review_text:
The apartment could be more clean. We observed several undefined stains on walls and bed linen. Bed linen were washed but some stains persisted.We found 2 cooking pots and 2 pans. The bigger cooking pot seemed inappropriate to cook and it would be convenient to have 2 correct cooking pots. Otherwise, the kitchen is sufficiently well equipped to cook for several days.Free wifi didn't work 3-4 first days, but host improved the situation afterwards. Guest who need good wifi for work (video call etc) should carefully consider their choice.2 chairs of table were unstable because screws were missing. And be careful if you like to open the window.In the morning, you hear easily in this apartment cars driving on the near high-traffic road Rue de la Loi.,

The location is very close to subway station Maelbeek and close to park Léopold and place Jourdan where several restaurants are located (Ramen, french fries/fritûre, ...). The host responds quickly by booking messages but he did not respond by phone (tried 1x). The access to the apartment was very easy and convenient.

I have to make a serious complaint about our stay in this room. The bed was full of bedbugs. The 1st morning we saw a spot of blood on the bed sheets and asked to change our sheets. It didn't cross our mind that something was wrong about the bed's hygiene. On Monday morning my husband got up with some spots/bites on his right hand and on his forehead. Yesterday morning he got up with many more bites on his right and left hand as well! We had to check out immediately so as to catch our train, therefore we didn't inform the receptionist on 5.40 am that we left. Since we arrived at our homeplace, we visited a doctor the same afternoon and the diagnosis is bites by bedbugs...We are really frustrated, disappointed and it's the 1st time that something like that happens to us! It is really concerning that in Brugges of 2020, Belgium, there are bedbugs in hotel rooms that are highly visited from all over the world.
